mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in triggered episodes are not statistically negative (mean > 0, p > 0.10), or the signal produces false breakouts in > 40% of out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing construction and residential fixed investment reflects robust consumer confidence and credit availability, driving higher aggregate demand and supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 60-day windows, or partial correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend, capacity utilization rises and corporate pricing power strengthens, leading to margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-production regimes are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.65), or the signal reverses direction during supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue streams, which mechanically supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat during GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation readings above the 240 index level force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy for extended periods, increasing the cost of capital and compressing equity multiples, thereby depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns in high-inflation regimes are not significantly negative, or the signal produces positive returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending without triggering inflationary wage spirals, supporting near-term equity gains.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain incremental variance in 5-day forward returns beyond individual series effects (F-test p > 0.05), or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: During a deceleration regime, slowing real GDP growth leads to earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as investors price in lower future cash flows.
failure_condition: Forward returns during GDP deceleration episodes are not statistically negative, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in > 60% of historical recessions.
